title: Galactorrhea and Amenorrhea - Prolactinoma
chief_complaint: 28-year-old with milky breast discharge (galactorrhea) and amenorrhea for 6 months. No periods since stopping OCPs 1 year ago. Occasional headaches. Not pregnant.
key_learning_points: MRI to assess size and chiasmal compression

patient_responses.periods: I haven't had a period in 6 months

feedback.incorrect_message: This is a prolactinoma. The ↑prolactin + MRI confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Microprolactinoma: Cabergoline 0.25mg twice weekly (titrate up). Normalizes prolactin in 80-90%. Tumor shrinkage in majority. Restores fertility. Follow prolactin q3 months. MRI annually. Stop treatment after 2 years if prolactin normal and tumor resolved.
